quoteFirst: But this is all as nothing now, for it is January 2nd, 1919, and at
quoteLast: this afternoon, after all hope of anything immediate had vanished, received orders for home.
title: A Doctor in France, 1917-1919: The Diary of Harold Barclay
author: Barclay, Harold, 1872-1922

quoteFirst: "You see, sir," he said saluting, "I'd been along the cuffs to Trimingham, and was on my way back about
quoteLast: , when I noticed a man lying yonder on the grass. It was a fine morning, quite light, and at first I thought it was a tramp, for they often sleep on the cliffs in the warm weather. But on going nearer I saw, to my surprise, that the man was old Mr. Gregory. I thought he was asleep, and bent down and shook him, his face being downwards on the grass and his arms stretched out. He didn't wake up, so I turned him over, and the colour of his face fair startled me. I opened his coat, put my hand on his heart, and found he was quite dead. I then ran along to our station
title: The Place of Dragons: A Mystery
author: Le Queux, William, 1864-1927

quoteFirst: We started this morning at seven, and at ten passed the large village of El Abadieh, a little below which, on the opposite side, is Engreyab; at a quarter past twelve, El Ferrakah, opposite which is Abselam; at half past twelve, El Dankel; at half past one, El Hassan (vestiges of a Saracenic castle), opposite which are the villages of Dekseet and Wady Shekeer; at half past one, the village of El Howe; at half past two, El Gadawab; at three, we passed a village called Housh, opposite which is Ellet Wady Gadallah; at
quoteLast: , Mahanifa; at half past three, Dich; and at four entered the capital, Makkarif.
title: Travels in Ethiopia, above the second cataract of the Nile
